title: SwarmKit Workspace
description: Deployment-level config: identity, governance, model providers, MCP registry, storage. See design §9.3 and design/details/workspace-schema-v1.md.

annotations.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata the runtime ignores. For enterprise use: cost_center, team, environment, compliance tags, etc.

policy_language.description: §21 open question — default yaml for v1.0.

limits.description: Circuit breaker thresholds. Prevents runaway execution and cost overruns.

max_steps_per_agent.description: Maximum execution steps per individual agent before abort.

max_steps_per_run.description: Maximum total steps across all agents in a single run. Default: 500.

max_cost_per_run_usd.description: Maximum estimated LLM cost (USD) per run before abort.

decision_skills.description: Mandatory decision skills that fire at specified trigger points. Topologies inherit these and can override by id.

credential_ref.$comment: Credentials are never stored literally in workspace.yaml. Each entry is a reference with a uniform shape: { source, config }. The runtime dispatches to the SecretsProvider identified by `source` and validates `config` against that provider's contract. See design note for the provider list and per-source config shapes. `source: plugin` additionally requires `provider_id` to identify a SecretsProvider registered via entry points.

provider_id.description: Required when source=plugin. Names the registered SecretsProvider.

config.description: Provider-specific configuration. Runtime validates shape per source.

env.description: Environment variables passed to a stdio server. Values support ${VAR} expansion from the runtime process environment. Use `credentials_ref` for secrets; `env` is for configuration.

cwd.description: Working directory for stdio servers. Supports ${VAR} expansion. Defaults to workspace root.

sandbox_image.description: Docker image for sandboxed servers. Defaults to swarmkit-mcp-sandbox (Python + mcp SDK). Use node:22-slim for Node.js servers.

permission.description: Governance permission tier for this server's tools. open: skip governance checks. cautious (default): reads auto-approved, writes go through governance. strict: all calls require explicit approval. readonly: write operations denied.

permission_overrides.description: Per-tool permission overrides. Keys are MCP tool names, values are permission tiers that override the server default.

runtime.description: Backend for jobs, conversations, and usage tracking. Defaults to sqlite at .swarmkit/store.sqlite.

backend.description: Storage backend. sqlite (default, zero config) or postgres (production, shared).

url.description: Connection URL for postgres backend. Supports ${ENV_VAR} interpolation. Ignored for sqlite.

context_compression.description: Opt-in read-side compression of bulk tool/MCP output before it re-enters an agent's context. Off by default. Never applied to the audit log or the inter-agent contract. Env vars SWARMKIT_CONTEXT_COMPRESSION and SWARMKIT_CONTEXT_COMPRESSION_MIN_BYTES override the default per deployment. See design/details/context-compression.md.

overrides.description: Per-surface rules matched by tool-name and/or server-id glob. The first override that matches wins; otherwise the top-level backend/min_bytes apply.

compression_backend.description: Compression backend. off (default): no compression. columnar: built-in lossless JSON minify + array-of-uniform-dicts rewrite to {columns, rows}. headtail: reversible-lossy — keep head+tail, elide the middle, recallable via the context_retrieve tool (for lossy-tolerant surfaces like logs). plugin: a custom ContextCompressor named by backend_class.

compression_backend_class.description: Fully-qualified Python class path of a custom ContextCompressor (used when backend=plugin), e.g. 'my_pkg.compressors.LearnedCompressor'.

compression_min_bytes.description: Payloads smaller than this (in characters) are left untouched — avoids overhead on small results.

compression_override.description: A per-surface compression rule. At least one of match / match_server is required.

match.description: Glob matched against the tool/skill name (e.g. 'get-logs', 'frigate*').

match_server.description: Glob matched against the MCP server id backing the tool (e.g. 'frigate', 'logs-*').

planning.description: Default planning behavior for all topologies in this workspace. Topology-level planning overrides these defaults.

scope_required.description: Leaders must call create-scope before synthesis.

two_phase.description: Enforce two-phase planning for all topologies.

synthesis.description: Automatic synthesis config. When set, the compiler invokes a large-context model directly with all research results instead of having the architect write the document.

provider.description: Model provider ID (e.g. google, openrouter, anthropic).

prompt.description: Custom system prompt for the synthesizer. Overrides the platform default. Use this to control document style, diagram generation (mermaid), grounding rules, and section handling.

decision_skill_binding.description: Binds a decision skill to a trigger point. Workspace bindings are inherited by all topologies; topology bindings can override by id.

id.description: Decision skill ID. Must exist in workspace skill registry.

trigger.description: When the skill fires: pre_input (before agent runs, validates user input), post_output (after agent output), checkpoint (between task batches), pre_synthesis (before final synthesis).

scope.description: Comma-separated agent IDs this binding applies to. Default '*' = all agents in the topology.

required.description: If true, output is rejected when this skill returns a failing verdict. Set false to disable an inherited workspace binding.

config.description: Skill-specific configuration (confidence thresholds, retry limits, etc).

server_config.description: Configuration for swarmkit serve mode. Controls job concurrency, timeouts, MCP server lifecycle, and canary deployments.

max_concurrent.description: Maximum number of concurrent topology executions.

enabled.description: Whether to start MCP servers at boot in serve mode.

canary.description: Canary deployment configuration. Routes traffic between topology versions by weight with optional auto-promotion. See design/details/canary-deployments.md.

routes.description: Canary routes. Each route splits traffic for one topology across multiple versions.

canary_route.description: Traffic splitting rule for a single topology.

topology.description: Topology name (matches metadata.name). Must exist in the workspace.

versions.description: Version entries. Weights must sum to 100.

canary_version.description: A single version in a canary route with its traffic weight and optional promotion criteria.

version.description: Topology version (semver). Must match a topology file's metadata.version.

weight.description: Percentage of traffic routed to this version (0-100).

promote_when.description: Auto-promotion criteria. When all conditions are met, this version is promoted to 100% traffic.

promote_criteria.description: Conditions that must ALL be met for auto-promotion of a canary version.

min_runs.description: Minimum number of completed runs before promotion is eligible.

error_rate_below.description: Maximum error rate (failed/total). E.g. 0.05 = 5% error rate threshold.

drift_below.description: Maximum average drift score. E.g. 0.30 = low drift tolerance.

window_minutes.description: Evaluation window in minutes. Only runs within this window count toward promotion criteria.
